achievements: Shipped Windows compatibility pass across backend (PATHEXT/cmd shim, reserved-name sanitize), frontend (platform.ts, path helpers), and bundle config (NSIS lzma, webview2 min version)

Built end-to-end voice control: VAD hysteresis, whisper.cpp Metal-prewarmed dictation, LocalAgreement-2 two-span render (committed + tentative), windowed incremental decode with 500ms watchdog, jarvis-as-boundary command parsing
Reverse skill bridge syncs ~/.claude/skills into ~/.terminal64/skills with symlinks, sidecar metadata, and Haiku-backfilled descriptions
Big-workflow stability sweep: PTY/provider/permission/widget cleanup paths, JSONL truncate-by-message audit fixes, canvas/claude/delegation store persistence with idle/quota fallback
Renderer hot-path patch: chat drag-drop routed by cursor proximity, guarded pan/zoom subscriptions, embedded widget-browser RAF loop replaced with event-coalesced ResizeObserver
Event parser hardening: top-level error handler, stream_request_start, thinking-block aware deltas, stop_reason surface for refusal/max_tokens
Codex MCP connected-status mapping + dropdown dedupe of T64 aliases across live/config entries
improvements: Dirty working tree across 22 files (browser_manager, lib.rs, permission_server, providers/*, pty_manager, widget_server, Canvas, ClaudeChat, XTerminal, BrowserPanel, WidgetPanel, useClaudeEvents, canvasStore, claudeStore, delegationStore) — split into reviewable commits before more work lands

lib.rs is ~70k tokens; the recent stability tests were placed there per buglog note — extract command handlers into per-domain modules (file_ops, history, checkpoints, theme, party) to reduce cognitive load and recompile times
Test coverage is thin given the surface area: only 8 intent tests + 7 command_stt tests + new JSONL/util guards. Add provider-event golden fixtures and a permission_server integration test

Voice subsystem has many tunable thresholds (VAD 0.5/0.35, silence frames 9, min_new_samples 120ms, decode watchdog 500ms) — promote to a single tuning struct + `voice_run_fixtures` regression run on PR
Memory/anatomy.md is dominated by venv contents — exclude scripts/.wake-venv/ from anatomy scanner to keep the index actionable
next_tasks: Stage and commit the 22 dirty files in 3-4 logical commits: (a) backend lifecycle cleanup, (b) frontend hot-path + store persistence, (c) provider/event/MCP fixes, (d) widget/browser panel patches
Run cargo clippy --all-targets across the dirty Rust files; the 03:19 entry noted Clippy was blocked by parallel edits — confirm clean now before pushing
Verify CI invariants locally: tsc --noEmit, cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -D warnings, cargo check for all 3 targets if possible
Backfill an integration test for the new permission_server pending-cleanup path (bug-046) — easy regression target
Add anatomy.md ignore rule for scripts/.wake-venv/** so future scans don't waste tokens on venv internals
Tag a release candidate once committed — release.yml is already wired for draft releases; signing is still TODO per CLAUDE.md so note that in the draft
risks: Unsigned macOS/Windows builds — Apple notarytool + Azure Trusted Signing secrets still missing; first-launch UX is right-click→Open. Decide whether to ship vN unsigned or hold for signing keys

voice/whisper.rs is flagged as load-bearing in CLAUDE.md (anti-hallucination reconciliation); recent windowed-decode + AgreementBuffer rewrite is large and lightly tested — high risk of subtle regression in long-utterance dictation

permission_server, widget_server, PTY spawning, and Discord bot are the exposed surfaces in SECURITY.md; recent lifecycle fixes touched all four — schedule a focused security review (or run /security-review) before tagging
claudeStore + delegationStore now have quota-fallback persistence with pruning logic — if pruning is too aggressive, users lose session metadata silently. Add telemetry/log when prune fires
lib.rs concentration is a bus factor + merge-conflict risk; the 03:30 entry already had a duplicate buglog id from parallel agent edits — likely to repeat
Codex provider (~55k tok) is the largest single file and changed recently; provider events, rollback/fork, and exec transport in one module is hard to reason about for incident response
